app.title: Tintpath
app.tagline: Turn any photo into a paint-by-numbers template — right in your browser.
app.privacy: 100% client-side. Your images never leave this device.

upload.formats: JPG, PNG or WEBP · up to 4000×4000 px
upload.errorFormat: Please use a JPG, PNG or WEBP image.
upload.errorLoad: The image could not be loaded.
upload.confirmLarge: This file is larger than 50 MB and may be slow to process. Load it anyway?
upload.resumeTitle: Pick up where you left off?
upload.sample: No photo handy? Try the sample image

crop.title: Crop your image and pick a paper size
crop.hint: Drag the frame to move it, drag a corner to resize. The aspect ratio follows the paper size.

detail.title: Choose the level of detail
detail.minRegionHint: Smaller regions are merged into the most similar neighbor.
detail.smoothingHint: How strongly the traced outlines are simplified.
detail.customPaletteHint: Match the paints you own — the color slider is ignored.

outline.title: Inspect the outlines
outline.canvasLabel: Outline drawing with numbered regions. Use the zoom buttons to inspect details.

colors.title: Adjust the palette
colors.hint: Click a swatch to swap a single color, or apply a preset to the whole palette.

export.title: Export your template
export.dxfHint: For laser software without SVG support
export.svgNumbersHint: Added as text elements — convert to paths in your laser software if needed.

imprint.title: Imprint
imprint.contactBody:
[Your name]

Replace this placeholder with your details before publishing the site.
imprint.privacyBody: Details on data protection are in the privacy policy. In short: images are processed only in your browser, nothing is uploaded, no cookies, no tracking.
imprint.disclaimerBody: Tintpath is free, open-source software (MIT license), provided “as is” and without warranty of any kind. You use it at your own risk and are responsible for holding the rights to the images you process. External links lead to third-party content for which the respective operators are responsible.

errors.pipeline: Processing failed. Try a smaller image or fewer colors.
errors.lowContrast: The image has very little contrast — try fewer colors.

footer.noUploads: No uploads, no accounts, no tracking

common.zoomHint: Scroll to zoom · drag to pan · double-click to toggle

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Tintpath is built to work without your data: images are processed entirely in your browser, there are no accounts, no tracking and no advertising. This page explains the little that remains.
privacy.controllerBody: Responsible for this website is the operator named in the imprint. For any privacy request, use the contact details given there.
privacy.noCookiesBody: This site sets no cookies and uses no analytics, tracking pixels or advertising services. That is why there is no cookie banner: under § 25 (2) TDDDG, consent is only required for storage that is not strictly necessary — everything Tintpath stores is functional (see below).
privacy.processingBody: Uploaded photos are decoded, edited and converted exclusively on your device. No image data, palettes or results are ever transmitted to a server.
privacy.storageBody:
Two kinds of data are stored locally in your browser, solely to provide features you actively use (Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR, § 25 (2) TDDDG):

· UI settings (language, theme, advanced mode) in localStorage under “tintpath:settings”.
· The resume feature saves your current session (source image and wizard settings) in IndexedDB so you can continue after a reload.

This data never leaves your device. You can delete it at any time via “Start over” / “Discard” in the app or through your browser’s site-data settings. The service worker additionally caches the app’s static files for offline use.
privacy.hostingBody:
When you open this website, the hosting provider technically needs to process your IP address to deliver the pages, and may keep short-lived access logs (IP address, date and time, requested URL, user agent) for security and stability (Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR).

Hosting provider: [Name and address of your hosting provider — replace before publishing.]
privacy.githubBody: The header shows the project’s GitHub star count. To fetch it, your browser sends one request to the public GitHub API (GitHub, Inc., USA), which technically includes your IP address. Legal basis is our legitimate interest in presenting the open-source project (Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR); the result is cached in your browser for one hour to minimize requests. See GitHub’s privacy statement for details on their processing.
privacy.rightsBody: Under Art. 15–21 GDPR you have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability and objection. Since Tintpath itself stores no personal data about you, there is usually nothing we could disclose or delete — everything lives in your own browser. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR).
